description: Canonical parameter dictionary for discrete diodes, synthesized from a real-datasheet corpus (Vishay, onsemi, Diodes Inc, MCC, Nexperia, Littelfuse, Wolfspeed, Central Semiconductor) spanning seven sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) standard silicon rectifiers (1N4007); (2) fast/ultrafast recovery rectifiers (UF400x, MURxxx) which populate reverse_recovery_time/charge; (3) Schottky rectifiers (1N5819, SS34, MBR0520L); (4) SiC Schottky (C3D06060A) which populate total_capacitive_charge/capacitance_stored_energy and a positive forward_voltage_temperature_coefficient; (5) small-signal switching diodes (1N4148, BAV99); (6) Zener / voltage-regulator diodes (1N4733A, BZX84C, MMSZ) which populate zener_voltage/zener_impedance (both swept over I_Z, the ZZT and ZZK points) and max_zener_current; and (7) transient-voltage-suppressor (TVS) diodes (SMAJ, SMBJ, 1.5KE) which populate reverse_standoff_voltage/clamping_voltage/peak_pulse_current/peak_pulse_power. A part's sub_type selects which optional keys apply; forward_voltage, reverse_leakage_current, junction_capacitance, thermal, and temperature keys are common. One-grade-per-document is strict here: a Zener series (dozens of VZ grades) or a TVS series (dozens of standoff grades) emits one document per orderable grade (its own mpn), with orderingVariants collapsing same-grade packaging codes. A bidirectional TVS (CA suffix) is component.polarity 'bipolar' with a single symmetric set of values, never signed. A dual-diode package (BAV99) disambiguates the two dice in the pinout (ANODE1/CATHODE1/ANODE2/CATHODE2, or a shared COMMON node), not with duplicate parameter arrays. Condition axes for this family: I_F (forward current), V_R (reverse/blocking voltage), I_T (breakdown test current), I_PP (peak pulse current), I_Z (Zener/test current, carrying the IZT and IZK points), I_RR (reverse-recovery measurement current), T_J, T_A, T_C, T_L (lead temperature), F (capacitance test frequency), R_L (recovery-test load), and WAVEFORM (pulse descriptor such as 10/1000us or 8.3ms half-sine, note-only). Pin functions are ANODE, CATHODE (and ANODE1/ANODE2/CATHODE1/CATHODE2/COMMON for arrays). Base-SI throughout; a printed %/degC breakdown tempco converts to ppm/degC (x10000), a mV/degC to V/degC; the I-squared-t fusing rating uses A2s. reverse_recovery_time/charge are first-class for fast and SiC parts and expected null on standard-Si and Schottky documents. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Peak repetitive reverse voltage
parameters.hint: The maximum recurring reverse voltage the diode blocks each cycle. Usually an absolute_max rating; for many rectifiers it equals the DC blocking voltage numerically but is a distinct rating.

parameters.name: Reverse standoff / working-peak voltage
parameters.hint: The maximum continuous reverse voltage with no significant conduction. On a TVS this is the headline operating spec (limitClass recommended) - the clamp stays off below it; on a rectifier it is the working-peak reverse voltage.

parameters.name: DC blocking voltage
parameters.hint: The maximum steady-state DC reverse voltage the diode blocks.

parameters.name: RMS reverse voltage
parameters.hint: The maximum RMS reverse voltage rating (roughly 0.7x the repetitive peak) for AC-line rectifiers.

parameters.name: Non-repetitive peak reverse voltage
parameters.hint: The maximum one-shot (non-repetitive) reverse voltage transient the diode survives.

parameters.name: Reverse breakdown voltage
parameters.hint: The reverse voltage at a stated breakdown test current (I_T for a TVS, I_R for a switching diode). On a TVS this is min/max at I_T; on a switching diode a minimum at a small I_R. For a Zener the equivalent is zener_voltage.

parameters.name: Forward voltage
parameters.hint: Forward voltage drop at a stated forward current (array over I_F) and junction temperature. Schottky/SiC parts give lower VF; note T_J since VF has a strong tempco. One measurement per (I_F, T_J) point.

parameters.name: Forward-voltage temperature coefficient
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the forward voltage. Negative for silicon PN/Schottky; positive for SiC Schottky (often stated qualitatively - capture only a tabulated value).

parameters.name: Peak forward recovery voltage
parameters.hint: The transient forward-voltage overshoot when the diode is switched on quickly at a stated di/dt and settling time.

parameters.name: Average rectified forward current
parameters.hint: The maximum average rectified forward current, rated at a reference temperature (lead T_L for axial/SMD, case T_C for power, ambient T_A). One measurement per reference-temperature point.

parameters.name: Continuous (DC) forward current
parameters.hint: The maximum continuous DC forward current at a stated reference temperature (common on power Schottky/SiC).

parameters.name: Repetitive peak forward current
parameters.hint: The maximum repetitive peak forward current, at a stated pulse width and reference temperature.

parameters.name: Non-repetitive peak forward surge current
parameters.hint: The single-cycle non-repetitive peak forward surge current, meaningless without its waveform - carry the pulse shape/width (8.3 ms half-sine, tp=1 us, etc.) as a WAVEFORM note. One measurement per waveform.

parameters.name: I-squared-t fusing rating
parameters.hint: The I-squared-t rating (ampere-squared-seconds) for fuse coordination, valid below a stated pulse width (typically t < 8.3 ms).

parameters.name: Reverse leakage current
parameters.hint: Reverse (blocking) leakage current at a stated reverse voltage (array over V_R) and junction temperature. Rises steeply with T_J, especially on Schottky/SiC - one measurement per (V_R, T_J) point.

parameters.name: Full-cycle-average reverse current
aliases: full load reverse current full cycle average
parameters.hint: The reverse current averaged over a full conduction cycle at a stated reference temperature (rectifier thermal budgeting).

parameters.name: Reverse recovery time
parameters.hint: Reverse recovery time at a stated forward current, di/dt, and recovery current. First-class for fast/ultrafast rectifiers; near-zero and usually untabulated for Schottky (majority-carrier) and SiC (zero-recovery) parts.

parameters.name: Reverse recovery charge
parameters.hint: Stored charge removed during reverse recovery, at a stated di/dt. Essentially zero on Schottky/SiC (a SiC part advertises 'zero reverse recovery' and gives total_capacitive_charge instead).

parameters.name: Junction / total capacitance
parameters.hint: Depletion (junction) capacitance at a stated reverse voltage (array over V_R) and test frequency (note F, typically 1 MHz). Falls with reverse bias - one measurement per V_R point.

parameters.name: Total capacitive charge
parameters.hint: SiC Schottky: the total charge stored in the junction capacitance to a stated reverse voltage - the switching-loss figure that replaces Qrr on a zero-recovery part.

parameters.name: Capacitance stored energy
parameters.hint: SiC Schottky: the energy stored in the junction capacitance at a stated reverse voltage.

parameters.name: Reverse-voltage rate of change (dV/dt)
parameters.hint: The maximum reverse-voltage rate of change the diode tolerates without spurious turn-on.

parameters.name: Clamping response time
parameters.hint: TVS: the time to clamp a fast transient (often stated only in prose/app notes).

parameters.name: Maximum clamping voltage
parameters.hint: TVS: the peak voltage across the device at a stated peak pulse current and waveform (array over I_PP). The design-critical let-through voltage - one measurement per I_PP/waveform point.

parameters.name: Maximum peak pulse current
parameters.hint: TVS: the maximum peak pulse current for a stated surge waveform (10/1000 us, 8/20 us) - carry the WAVEFORM note. Meaningless without it.

parameters.name: Peak pulse power dissipation
parameters.hint: TVS: the peak pulse power the device dissipates for a stated surge waveform (the headline TVS rating, e.g. 400 W at 10/1000 us). One measurement per waveform.

parameters.name: Breakdown-voltage temperature coefficient
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the reverse breakdown (Zener VZ or TVS VBR). Store ppm/degC (a printed %/degC converts x10000); a printed absolute mV/degC uses the V/degC altUnit (converts /1000). Sign flips from negative to positive around ~5-6 V breakdown.

parameters.name: Zener (breakdown) voltage
parameters.hint: Zener: the regulated breakdown voltage at the Zener test current IZT (array over I_Z). min/typ/max come from the grade tolerance (the A/B/C suffix), all at the same IZT - one grade per document.

parameters.name: Zener dynamic impedance
parameters.hint: Zener: small-signal impedance, intrinsically a two-point array over I_Z - ZZT at the test current IZT and ZZK at the knee current IZK (~100x higher). Never merge the two points.

parameters.name: Maximum Zener current
parameters.hint: Zener: the maximum continuous DC Zener (regulator) current, set by the power rating divided by VZ.

parameters.name: Non-repetitive peak Zener surge current
parameters.hint: Zener: the one-shot peak reverse (Zener) surge current at a stated pulse width.

parameters.name: Total power dissipation
parameters.hint: Maximum total power dissipation, rated at a reference temperature (lead T_L, ambient T_A, or case T_C). One measurement per reference-temperature point - a consumer must not apply a case-cooled figure in free air.

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance (K/W and degC/W are identical; normalize to degC/W).

parameters.name: Junction-to-lead thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-lead thermal resistance (axial/SMD parts, at a stated lead length or mounting).

parameters.name: Junction-to-case thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-case thermal resistance (power packages).

parameters.name: Junction-to-solder-point thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-solder-point thermal resistance (SMD parts characterized to the PCB pad).

parameters.name: Junction temperature (maximum)
parameters.hint: Maximum operating junction temperature (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: The operating (junction) temperature range (min/max).

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range (min/max).
